results.possibleIdentity: Perrin C. White is a distinguished pediatric endocrinologist and biochemist known for his pioneering research on the molecular genetics of steroid hormone synthesis. Born around 1950, he attended Forest Hills High School in Queens, New York, where he was a top winner in the 1968 Westinghouse Science Talent Search. He subsequently attended Harvard University, where he earned his Bachelor of Arts (A.B.) and his Doctor of Medicine (M.D.) in 1976. During his time at Harvard in the late 1960s and early 1970s, he specialized in biochemistry and was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which facilitated collaborations between technical professionals and artists. His early career included a postdoctoral fellowship at Rockefeller University under Nobel laureate Gerald Edelman and pediatrics training at Johns Hopkins and New York Hospital. In 1981, he joined the faculty of Cornell University Medical College, where he successfully cloned the cDNA for human steroid 21-hydroxylase, the enzyme deficient in most cases of congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH). He later moved to the University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center, where he has served as the Audrey Newman Rapoport Distinguished Chair in Pediatric Endocrinology. His work has extensively covered the genetics and biochemistry of adrenal disorders and the development of the bionic pancreas for diabetes management.
